column1: # Golden ordering corpus for the Gem::Version comparator.
# Format: <a> TAB <b> TAB <sign>, where <sign> is cmp(a, b): -1 (a<b), 0 (a==b), 1 (a>b).
# Lines beginning with '#' and blank lines are ignored.
# These encode MODERN RubyGems (>= 3.3.0) `Gem::Version#<=>` semantics, which compares
# `canonical_segments` (trailing zeros of the numeric prefix and of the prerelease tail
# dropped). RubyGems <= 3.2 compared the raw `segments` and disagrees on the marked
# CANONICAL-VS-RAW cases below. This comparator deliberately targets the modern rule, so
# the marked rows lock in the modern result; do NOT "fix" them to match an old system Ruby.
# Provenance: derived from the canonical_segments algorithm and cross-checked against the
# documented RubyGems spec. Regenerate/extend with `examples/vercmp.rs` against Ruby >= 3.3.
# --- basic numeric ordering ---
# --- trailing-zero insignificance (canonical equality) ---
# --- prereleases sort below their release; string segment < numeric segment ---
1.0.0.beta
1.0.0.beta
1.0.0.alpha
# --- alphanumeric segments split letter/number; the number compares numerically ---
# --- platform suffix (everything from the first '-') is irrelevant to the version ---
1.0.0-java
1.0.0.beta1-x86_64-linux
# --- CANONICAL-VS-RAW: differing-length numeric cores under a prerelease tag.
# Modern (canonical_segments): 1.0.0.beta1 -> [1,"beta",1], 1.0.beta2 -> [1,"beta",2] => a<b.
# Old RubyGems (<=3.2, raw segments) gave a>b. We pin the modern result.
